title: Red Sox place Trevor Story on injured list with sports hernia, contemplating surgery
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox placed Trevor Story on the 10-day injured list with a sports hernia, considering surgery.
Nick Sogard is recalled from Triple-A Worcester to replace Story on the active roster.
Story, struggling with a .206 average and six errors, may be replaced by Andruw Monasterio or Marcelo Mayer.

title: One driver dead in NH collision between pickup truck and school bus
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A 74-year-old driver, David Peloski, died in a head-on crash with a school bus in Marlborough, NH.
The crash involved a school bus carrying 13 students; none sustained serious injuries, and all were safely reunited with families.
The incident on Route 12 remains under investigation by NH State Police and involved units. Witnesses are urged to contact authorities.

title: Carl Yastrzemski’s grandson, Braves’ Mike Yastrzemski, walks off Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox lost 3-2 to the Braves, with Mike Yastrzemski's walk-off hit ending the game in the 10th inning.
Boston's Marcelo Mayer hit a solo home run to tie the game 2-2 in the seventh inning.
Starting pitcher Connelly Early gave up two solo homers in five innings, striking out six without issuing a walk.

title: Report: Reunion between Patriots, Stefon Diggs viewed as unlikely despite mutual interest
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Stefon Diggs is interested in returning to the Patriots, but a trade for A.J. Brown hinders the possibility.
The Patriots are expected to take on A.J. Brown's $32 million salary, complicating a potential Diggs reunion.
With Brown, Doubs, and others, the Patriots' offense aims to improve in 2026, but Diggs re-signing is unlikely.

title: Boston police seek missing boy, 15
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: 15-year-old Lafayette LaGrant-Gomes was last seen Tuesday around 1 p.m. near 965 Blue Hill Ave. in Dorchester.
Boston police describe him as a Black male, 5'8'', with a thin build, brown eyes, and black hair.
Public is urged to contact District B-3 detectives or submit anonymous tips if they have information on his whereabouts.

title: Police: Teen allegedly shot girlfriend’s mother in the head on Mother’s Day
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Tyler Broomes, 18, was arrested for allegedly shooting his girlfriend's mother and another man in Dorchester on May 10.
The 42-year-old woman sustained life-threatening injuries; a 42-year-old man was also wounded but not critically.
Broomes faces multiple charges, including assault with intent to murder; a dangerousness hearing is scheduled for May 21.

title: How would a team made up entirely of former Red Sox look in 2026?
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Former Red Sox starting pitchers, including Chris Sale and Nathan Eovaldi, have a combined 2.74 ERA in 39 starts this season.
Ex-Red Sox players like Mookie Betts and Xander Bogaerts remain productive contributors to their current teams in 2026.
A hypothetical 2026 team of former Red Sox includes notable names like Rafael Devers and Kyle Schwarber, providing strong offensive potential.

title: Red Sox’ Roman Anthony no longer wearing wrist brace, played catch Friday
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox outfielder Roman Anthony removed his wrist brace after a cortisone injection, following a right wrist sprain.
Anthony played catch ahead of Friday's game against Atlanta, but has not yet resumed swinging a bat.
Anthony has missed nine games since his injury on May 4 and remains optimistic about a return.

title: Jeep drives into Norwood day care
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A Jeep crashed into BrightPath daycare in Norwood Friday morning, causing the building to close temporarily for damage assessment.
No injuries were reported as children, staff, and the driver were unharmed; all children were safely evacuated.
The driver mistakenly accelerated into the building while attempting to stop the SUV from rolling; structural damage deemed 'moderate.'

title: Here’s what experts are predicting the Patriots’ 2026 record will be
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Experts predict the New England Patriots will finish the 2026 season with a 10-7 record, down from 14-3 in 2025.
Challenges include games against Seattle, Denver, and Chicago, contributing to a tougher schedule than the previous year.
Some predictions suggest playoff contention with key games against teams like Jacksonville, Chicago, and Green Bay.
